instructions:
You are connected to the 'ntfy' host via rrsh, a read-only diagnostic gateway. The `list_commands` response you just received includes the full allowlist in its `commands` field - each rule's `command` field is a list of regexes (element 0 matches the binary path, elements 1..N-1 match the request's `command` elements 1-for-1). Your request's `command` length must equal the rule's `command` length AND each element must match its corresponding pattern. Multiple rules can share a command[0] regex to describe alternative command shapes (e.g. `ps aux` and `ps -ef` are two separate rules). For any rule whose 'as' field lists a non-$USER user (e.g. 'root'), you MUST pass {"as":"root"} (or whichever user) explicitly on the run_command call - rrsh does not auto-elevate. Use `run_command` with `{command: [...]}` for a single command or `run_pipeline` with `{pipeline: [{command: [...]}, ...]}` to chain stages.

LOG LAYOUT on this host:
  /var/log/ntfy.log                    - main ntfy server log (current)
  /var/log/ntfy.log.N.gz               - daily rotations, all gzipped (logrotate has no delaycompress for ntfy)
  /var/log/ntfy-goroutine.log          - periodic goroutine dumps written by emergency-restart cron (every 2 min)
  /var/log/ntfy-health-check.log       - synthetic health-check probe output (cron, every 2 min)
  /var/log/ntfy-database-backup.log    - hourly backup script output
  /var/log/nginx/access.log            - 2xx/3xx HTTP responses
  /var/log/nginx/access.log.1          - yesterday's, uncompressed; .2.gz onwards are gzipped
  /var/log/nginx/access_error.log      - 4xx/5xx HTTP responses (scan/attack noise lands here)
  /var/log/nginx/access_error.log.N    - same rotation pattern (.1 uncompressed, .N.gz compressed)
  /var/log/nginx/error.log             - nginx's own error log + rotations
  /var/log/auth.log                    - SSH attempts, sudo activity, login session events
  /var/log/syslog                      - general system log

For uncompressed .log and .log.1 files, use cat/tail/head/grep/wc. For gzipped rotations (*.gz), use zcat or zgrep - ztail and zhead do NOT exist, so use a pipeline like `zcat /var/log/ntfy.log.5.gz | tail -n 200`.

For systemd unit logs (live, in-memory journal), use journalctl with -u {ntfy,nginx,ssh,sshd}. systemctl status / is-active inspects unit state.

fail2ban-client status shows active jails and currently-banned IPs. lastb shows failed SSH attempts, last shows successful logins.

For Prometheus metrics, run `ip addr` first to find the host's 10.x address, then `curl -sS http://10.X.Y.Z:9090/metrics` or `:9100/metrics`. 9090 typically exposes the app's own /metrics (e.g. ntfy_*); 9100 is node_exporter for host-level CPU/IO/disk/network. Neither binds to localhost.

For disk-usage diagnosis, `du -sh <path>` is allowed under /var/log and /var/lib (or any non-dotfile subpath). Cron job definitions live under /etc/cron.d/ and /etc/crontab - list with `ls`, read with `cat`. Custom diagnostic/maintenance scripts (e.g. the synthetic health-check probe) live under /usr/local/bin/ - also readable with `cat`/`head`. journalctl also accepts `--grep <regex>` for filtering noisy unit logs.

No write access. If a command you want isn't allowed, stop and tell the operator rather than trying to work around the matcher.

commands.description: Show the effective username.
Show the user/group identity (uid, gid, groups). No args.
Print the system hostname.
Show how long the system has been up and the load average.
Print kernel/OS info. Only `-a` is permitted.
List processes (BSD-style: ps aux).
List processes with forest view (ps auxf).
List processes (System V-style: ps -ef).
List processes with custom format. Allowed columns: pid, user, comm, stat, etime, pcpu, pmem, rss, vsz, tty, nice. Avoids the `args`/`command` columns that would leak full argv.
Take one batch-mode snapshot of top (`-bn1`).
Show the process tree.
Report memory usage. No args = default human-readable layout.
Report memory usage with a specific unit (`-h`, `-m`, or `-g`).
Report filesystem space usage. No args = default columns.
Report filesystem space usage with `-h` (human-readable) or `-i` (inodes).
Summarize disk usage under /var/log (or a subpath that does not start with `.`). Runs as root so it can traverse mode-750 log subdirs (e.g. /var/log/private). Blocks `..` traversal.

Summarize disk usage under /var/lib (or a subpath that does not start with `.`). Runs as root so it can traverse mode-700 app data dirs (e.g. /var/lib/ntfy). Recursive on large trees; allow up to 60s.
List block devices.
Show network state (one-word subcommands): addr, route, link. Accepts any of the standard `ip` binary paths so the call works regardless of usrmerge / distro layout.
List listening sockets with PIDs. Allowed flag combos: -tlnp, -ulnp, -tulnp.
Ping a host a fixed number of times: -c <count> <host>. Host pattern excludes leading dots/dashes to discourage internal-net probing.
DNS lookup via system resolver (single hostname). No @server allowed - would be a DNS exfil channel.
DNS lookup of a specific record type. No @server.

Fetch a Prometheus metrics endpoint on the host's private 10.x interface (NOT localhost). 9090 typically exposes the app's own /metrics (e.g. ntfy_*); 9100 is node_exporter (host CPU/IO/disk/network). Use `ip addr` first to discover the right address.
Show recent successful logins from /var/log/wtmp. No args = full list.
Show the last N successful logins.
Show recent FAILED login attempts from /var/log/btmp. No args = full list.
Show the last N failed login attempts. Useful for spotting SSH brute-force activity.
List active fail2ban jails.
Show currently-banned IPs in a specific fail2ban jail (e.g. sshd, nginx-400).
Show full systemd journal for ntfy, nginx, ssh, or sshd.
Show the systemd journal for ntfy, nginx, ssh, or sshd with --no-pager (better for scripted reads).
Show the last N entries for ntfy, nginx, ssh, or sshd.
Show entries since <expr> (any time-spec journalctl accepts, e.g. '1 hour ago').
Filter a unit's journal by case-sensitive regex (PCRE).
Filter the last N journal entries by regex. Useful for finding specific error patterns in a noisy unit log.
Filter journal entries by regex within a time window.
Tail the last N lines of an uncompressed log file under /var/log. Covers ntfy*.log, nginx/*.log, syslog, auth.log, plus their .1 rotations. For .gz use zcat | tail.
Read the first N lines of an uncompressed log file.
Read the first N lines of a script or file under /usr/local/bin (filename starts with a word char or dash, no `..`).
Dump an uncompressed log file. For .gz use zcat.
Read the system crontab.
Read a cron job file under /etc/cron.{d,hourly,daily,weekly,monthly}. Useful for understanding what scheduled scripts run (e.g. ntfy-health-check, emergency-restart).
Dump a script or text file under /usr/local/bin. Useful for inspecting custom diagnostic/maintenance scripts referenced by cron.
Dump a gzipped log rotation (*.log.N.gz). Pipe through tail/head for the last/first N lines.
List /var/log or a subpath. Runs as root to peek into mode-750 subdirs. Blocks `..` in the path.
List /var/lib or a subpath. Runs as root to peek into mode-700 app data dirs (e.g. /var/lib/ntfy). Blocks `..` in the path.
List entries in /etc/cron.{d,hourly,daily,weekly,monthly}. Useful for discovering scheduled jobs before reading them with `cat`.
List entries under /usr/local/bin. Useful for discovering custom diagnostic/maintenance scripts.
Count lines in an uncompressed log file.
Case-insensitive grep against an uncompressed log file: `-i <pattern> <path>`. For .gz use zgrep.
Case-insensitive grep, intended as a pipeline stage (filter output from a previous allowlisted command).
Case-insensitive extended-regex grep, intended as a pipeline stage (filter output from a previous allowlisted command).
Case-insensitive grep against a gzipped log rotation.
Inspect a systemd unit's runtime state (status or is-active for ntfy or nginx).
List systemd timers.
Inspect ntfy state. Permitted: `user list`, `access list`, `tier list`.
